msgs.text:
Turn 16 — C9=L9; degree 18 and 19 clear The target is still the over-G isomorphism problem for the two quotients ΓS,ΓH, with the required condition πH∘Φ=πS, as stated in the attached problem statement.The previous turn reduced the next obstruction to the residual mod-2, s=1, Ef-piece and left exactly one condition to check:C9=L9,where C9 is the space of degree-9 residual tails that can be introduced and then continued through I18. If C9=L9, then the degree-18 square quotientQ18=L18/im∂17≅L9[2]is hit by the transgression W↦W[2], and degree 18 dies. This was the precise gap left at the end of Turn 15.

The new result is:C9=L9.So the degree-18 obstruction dies, and degree 19 then dies formally.The reason is not a new 56-coordinate computation. It is the same uniform pattern that has been emerging since degree 10: do not inspect a fixed tail; inspect the full affine kernel preserving the lower layers. Degree 10 was killed by the degree-8 kernel freedom onto L5[2]; degree 12 and degree 14 were killed by the full affine kernels rather than by the nearest naive kernel; and degree 16 only died after the lower Y4 -base deformation supplied the missing high-torsor direction.Here is the clean degree-9 tail argument.Let∂8:L8⊕L8→L9,(U,V)↦[A,U]+[B,V].

A degree-8 correction placed on Y1 and q, with initial forms U,V, changes the residual expression byR(Y0,Y1,q)=S(Y0,Y1,q)X−2as

R⟼1+∂8 (U,V)+O(I10).

Turn 6’s odd-layer mechanism says that for odd n,Ln=[A,Ln−1]+[B,Ln−1],so in particular im∂8=L9.

Thus every W∈L9 can be introduced as a temporary degree-9 tail. The only possible worry is that continuation through degrees 10,…,17 might cut this image down. It does not. The subsequent obstruction layers are exactly the already-treated square layers:10121416:L5[2],:L6[2],:L7[2],:L8[2].For degrees 10,12,14, the relevant full affine kernels were already shown to hit the whole square quotient. For degree 16, the high torsor alone missed one direction, but Turn 14 showed that the lower Y0↦Y0Y4 deformation toggles exactly that missing direction. Therefore, after prescribing an arbitrary W∈L9, the continuation equations through I18 remain solvable. HenceC9=im∂8=L9.This is the structural point of the turn: degree 18 is not a new fixed-gauge obstruction; it is the square of an odd boundary layer, and the odd boundary layer is fully continuable.Now apply the Turn 15 transgression. SinceQ18=L18/im∂17≅L9[2],and C9=L9, the mapT9:C9→Q18,W↦W[2]is onto. Modulo im∂17, it is linear because in characteristic 2,(W+W′)[2]=W[2]+W′[2]+[W,W′],and the cross-bracket [W,W′]∈L18 is ordinary Lie material, hence dies in the square quotient Q18. Thus any actual degree-18 square class can be shifted into im∂17, then killed by a degree-17 correction.

So there is a residual section-twisted lift withS(Y0,Y1,q)≡X2 (modI19).Degree 19 is odd, so Turn 6’s primitive-boundary argument applies again:L19=[A,L18]+[B,L18].

After a degree-18 correction, the congruence improves toS(Y0,Y1,q)≡X2 (modI20).

This still does not proveΓS≅GΓH.But degree 18 is now cleared uniformly, not merely by a fixed-tail computation. The next genuinely dangerous layer is degree 20, withQ20=L20/im∂19≅L10[2],dimL10=105.

Unlike degree 18, the root L10 is even and already contains restricted-square material L5[2]. So degree 20 is not just another odd-root transgression; it is the next real combined-torsor test, with interactions such as 8+12, 9+11, and 10+10.

ESTABLISHED: C9=L9. Hence the degree-18 square quotient Q18≅L9[2]is fully hit by the odd-root transgression W↦W[2], so degree 18 dies. Degree 19 then clears by the odd-layer primitive-boundary argument. The residual congruence is solvable throughS(Y0,Y1,q)≡X2 (modI20).

GAP: This is still not a complete proof of ΓS≅GΓH. The next possible obstruction is degree 20, where the square quotient is L10[2]of dimension 105, and L10 is an even restricted layer rather than an odd boundary layer.

NEXT: Build the degree-20 combined torsor. Do not test a fixed degree-20 tail. Track the full lower-plus-high affine kernel through I20, including 8+12, 9+11, 10+10, and the lower-base transgressions, and test whether it hits all ofL20/im∂19≅L10[2].[DEG18-C9-FULL-DEG19-CLEARED]
